originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&argv` is never used.

reasoning: Line 16: `p = *argv++, --argc` — the post-increment of argv stores a new value in argv that is never read again (dead store of argv). This statement is in main() outside any #ifdef, so the dead store occurs in all configurations (when 'W' case is hit and r is truthy). No '// ERROR' for the argv dead store → new bug not in VBDB. (The ERROR at L21 is for the uninitialized 'p' bug, a separate known issue.) PC requires UNREGISTER=F AND SCAN=F but the dead store exists regardless of these flags. PC is over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
